analysis_note: This comparison aggregates existing manifest-bound scheduled replay summaries. It does not re-parse raw receiver logs or infer exact transmitted-packet counts, confirmed collisions, true latency, live belief-controller behavior, or airtime optimization.

per_transmitter.role: loose usefulness-threshold schedule replayed from microSD
medium usefulness-threshold 64-row schedule replayed from microSD

comparison.interpretation: Observed received-packet ratio should be interpreted as a proportion consistent with scheduled skipping, not as a confirmed transmitted-packet or collision count.

per_run.interpretation: Run 024 shows that the observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio is close to the scheduled send-fraction ratio, consistent with scheduled skipping, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet.
cautions: This is point-to-point LoRa at 915 MHz, not LoRaWAN.
The schedule CSVs define one repeated schedule period.
The analyzer compares schedule proportions and observed packet proportions.
The analyzer does not infer exact transmitted packet counts.
Missing sequence numbers are observed sequence gaps, not confirmed collisions.
recv_ms and tx_ms are not synchronized across boards and should not be interpreted as true latency.
Usefulness and priority are synthetic metadata in this milestone.
The run does not yet use a live belief-maintenance controller.
Use the wording reduced physical transmission attempts under scheduled skipping, not airtime optimization.

per_run.interpretation: Run 026 uses a stricter usefulness-threshold schedule than Runs 024 and 025: TXB schedules 4/16 SEND rows rather than 8/16. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio is consistent with the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet.

per_run.interpretation: Run 027 uses a looser usefulness-threshold schedule than Runs 024, 025, and 026: TXB schedules 12/16 SEND rows. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio is consistent with the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet than TXA. Run 027 fills the loose-threshold point in the threshold-family ladder.

comparison.interpretation: Observed received-packet ratio should be interpreted as a proportion consistent with scheduled skipping, not as a confirmed transmitted-packet or collision count.

per_run.interpretation: Run 028 replays the Run 027-style loose-threshold schedule from microSD rather than compiled firmware headers. TXA/N01 loaded a fixed-all 16/16 SEND schedule and TXB/N16 loaded a loose-threshold 12/16 SEND schedule from /schedule.csv. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio was close to the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet than TXA. This supports a bounded storage-mechanism interpretation: the scheduled-skipping semantics can move from compiled headers to microSD-backed replay under similar two-transmitter lab conditions.
cautions: This is point-to-point LoRa at 915 MHz, not LoRaWAN.

Run 028 changes the storage and replay mechanism, not the underlying schedule semantics.

comparison.interpretation: Observed received-packet ratio should be interpreted as a proportion consistent with scheduled skipping, not as a confirmed transmitted-packet or collision count.

per_run.interpretation: Run 029 extends microSD-backed scheduled replay from a 16-row schedule to a 64-row two-transmitter schedule. TXA/N01 loaded a fixed-all 64/64 SEND schedule and TXB/N16 loaded a medium usefulness-threshold 32/64 SEND schedule from /schedule.csv. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio was close to the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet than TXA. This supports a bounded longer-schedule SD replay interpretation under similar two-transmitter lab conditions.
cautions: This is point-to-point LoRa at 915 MHz, not LoRaWAN.
